name: claudemeter
description: Monitor your Claude Code usage proactively in real time, with full limit information. No more 'Surprise! You've hit your Claude Code weekly limit and it resets in 3 days you lucky, lucky person!'. Tracks session, weekly, and token limits across all Claude plans.

scripts.package: node esbuild.js --production && rm -f dist/*.map && vsce package
scripts.publish: node esbuild.js --production && rm -f dist/*.map && vsce publish

colors.description: Red used for outage states and over-threshold gauges. Tuned to match the platform error background tone. Override via workbench.colorCustomizations.
Light blue used for the Tk 'context drift' tier (~300K tokens used on 1M+ profiles). Subdued Solarized cyan. Override via workbench.colorCustomizations or claudemeter.colors.rotLight.
Dark blue used for the Tk 'context rot' tier (~500K tokens used on 1M+ profiles). Subdued Solarized blue. Override via workbench.colorCustomizations or claudemeter.colors.rotDeep.
Subdued green used for the happy-hour status-bar panel. Gruvbox green. Override via workbench.colorCustomizations or claudemeter.colors.happyHour.

claudemeter.fetchOnStartup.description: Automatically fetch usage data when VS Code starts

claudemeter.autoRefreshMinutes.description: Auto-refresh interval in minutes for fetching Claude.ai usage data (1-60 minutes)

claudemeter.localRefreshSeconds.description: Local token refresh interval in seconds (5-60 seconds). Controls how often local Claude Code token data is polled from JSONL files. Set higher to reduce CPU usage.

enumDescriptions: Full display: Claude Se 13% ⏱ 11:59  Wk 11% ⏱ Fri 11  Tk 36%
Percentages only: Claude Se 13%  Wk 11%  Tk 36%
Single compact panel: Claude S-13% Wk-11% Tk-36%
claudemeter.statusBar.displayMode.markdownDescription:
Status bar display mode:
- **default**: Full display with reset times (separate panels)
- **minimal**: Percentages only (separate panels)
- **compact**: All metrics in a single panel

claudemeter.statusBar.showSonnet.description: Show Sonnet weekly usage in status bar (default/minimal modes only)

claudemeter.statusBar.showOpus.description: Show Opus weekly usage in status bar (Max plans only, default/minimal modes)

claudemeter.statusBar.showCredits.description: Show extra usage (spending cap) in status bar (default/minimal modes only)

claudemeter.statusBar.showServiceStatus.description: Show Claude service status indicator. When enabled, displays a warning/error icon if Claude services are degraded or experiencing an outage.

claudemeter.statusBar.timeFormat.markdownDescription: How to display reset times in the status bar

claudemeter.statusBar.alignment.description: Status bar alignment (left or right side). Requires window reload to take effect.

claudemeter.statusBar.priority.description: Status bar priority. Higher values position items further from the screen edge (left, for right-aligned items). Default 10000 keeps the cluster contiguous and well clear of VS Code core items (overtype/encoding/etc., which live around priority 100). Requires window reload to take effect.

claudemeter.statusBar.usageFormat.markdownDescription:
How to display usage values in the status bar.

**Platform-aware default**: when this setting is not explicitly configured, Windows uses `barLight` (Block Elements render reliably in the default Windows status-bar font), while macOS and Linux use `barCircle`. Override here to force a specific style on any platform — `barLight` and `barSolid` use Block Elements and are the safest fallback if your chosen style renders unevenly.

enumDescriptions: Progress bar + max only when extended beyond 200K (e.g. Tk ●●○○○ 1m on a 1M session; just the bar on a 200K session) — default
Token count only, no bar (e.g. Tk 518k/1m)
claudemeter.statusBar.tokensDisplay.markdownDescription:
How to display the **Tk** (token usage) item in the status bar:

- **bar**: progress bar / percent only — `Tk ●●○○○`
- **value**: bar + current consumption — `Tk ●●○○○ 518k`
- **extended**: bar + current/max — `Tk ●●○○○ 518k/1m` (this was the pre-2.3.3 `both` default)
- **limit** _(default)_: bar + max, shown only when greater than the 200K standard context window — `Tk ●●○○○ 1m` on a 1M session; just the bar on a 200K session
- **count**: token count only, no bar — `Tk 518k/1m`

When the context window limit is inferred rather than authoritative, the max is suppressed to avoid misrepresenting an uncertain value.

_Backward compatibility:_ the legacy `both` value is auto-migrated to `extended` on startup so any existing configuration keeps its previous look._

claudemeter.thresholds.warning.markdownDescription: Global usage percentage to show the warning (yellow) indicator. Per-gauge overrides take precedence — see `claudemeter.thresholds.{session,weekly,tokens,sonnet,opus,credits}.warning`.

claudemeter.thresholds.error.markdownDescription: Global usage percentage to show the error (red) indicator. Per-gauge overrides take precedence — see `claudemeter.thresholds.{session,weekly,tokens,sonnet,opus,credits}.error`.

claudemeter.thresholds.tokens.warning.markdownDescription: Token-gauge warning (yellow) threshold. Default `65` tracks Claude Code's auto-compact trigger (~83% of the context window, gated by `CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE`) minus ~20 — so the gauge turns yellow ~20 percentage points before auto-compact. `0` inherits `claudemeter.thresholds.warning`.

claudemeter.thresholds.tokens.error.markdownDescription: Token-gauge error (red) threshold. Default `75` tracks Claude Code's auto-compact trigger (~83% of the context window) minus ~10 — giving roughly 10 percentage points of red runway before auto-compact. `0` inherits `claudemeter.thresholds.error`.

claudemeter.statusBar.colorMode.markdownDescription:
Status-bar decoration mode.

- **color** (default): full palette — gauge tiers (rotLight, rotDeep, warning, error), Se/Wk warning icons, happy-hour green, and the colour-coded `●` prefix in the tooltip.
- **basic**: every gauge renders in default foreground; no icons; no tier tints; no tooltip prefix dots; no rot recommendation sub-lines. Tooltip section structure (Session / Current context / Weekly) is preserved either way.

claudemeter.thresholds.tokens.profileOve.markdownDescription: Force a specific Tk profile by name (e.g. `max-20x`, `pro`, `enterprise`). Empty (default) auto-detects from your Claude account signals. Built-in profile names: `pro`, `max-5x`, `max-20x`, `max-unknown`, `team-standard`, `enterprise`, `unknown`.

claudemeter.colors.rotLight.markdownDescription: Custom hex (e.g. `#6ca0c4`) overriding the rotLight tier colour. Empty (default) inherits from the `claudemeter.rotLight` theme colour. Setting this here is theme-agnostic.

claudemeter.colors.rotDeep.markdownDescription: Custom hex overriding the rotDeep tier colour. Empty inherits from the `claudemeter.rotDeep` theme colour. Theme-agnostic.

claudemeter.colors.warning.markdownDescription: Custom hex overriding the warning (yellow) tier colour. Empty inherits from `charts.yellow`.

claudemeter.colors.error.markdownDescription: Custom hex overriding the error (red) tier colour. Empty inherits from `claudemeter.outageRed`.

claudemeter.colors.happyHour.markdownDescription: Custom hex overriding the happy-hour panel colour. Empty inherits from `claudemeter.happyHourGreen` (Gruvbox green by default).

claudemeter.debug.description: Enable debug logging to output channel (for troubleshooting)

claudemeter.happyHour.enabled.markdownDescription:
Show a 'happy hour' icon on the Claude status-bar panel during Anthropic's off-peak window (when their peak-throttling policy is not active).

Peak window defaults to Mon-Fri 05:00-23:00 America/Los_Angeles, per Anthropic's 2026-03-26 announcement. Configurable via `claudemeter.happyHour.peakWindow`.

Anthropic throttling is driven by LA-local peak load — this icon appears outside that window regardless of your own timezone.

claudemeter.happyHour.icon.markdownDescription: Icon shown during happy hour. The first four options are monochrome VS Code codicons that inherit the status-bar text colour (subtle, unobtrusive). The remainder are full-colour emoji. Set to `custom` and provide `claudemeter.happyHour.customIcon` to use any glyph (emoji or `$(codicon-name)` syntax).

claudemeter.happyHour.customIcon.markdownDescription: Custom emoji for happy hour (used only when `claudemeter.happyHour.icon` is `custom`).

days.description: Days of week where peak applies. 0=Sunday, 1=Monday, ..., 6=Saturday.

start.description: Peak window start time in HH:MM 24-hour format, in `tz`.

end.description: Peak window end time in HH:MM 24-hour format, in `tz`.

claudemeter.happyHour.peakWindow.markdownDescription:
Anthropic's announced peak-throttling window. Happy hour shows OUTSIDE this window.

Defaults to `{ days: [1,2,3,4,5], start: "05:00", end: "23:00", tz: "America/Los_Angeles" }` per Anthropic's 2026-03-26 announcement. Override here if the policy changes.

Malformed fields fall back to the defaults individually (e.g. a bad `tz` still keeps your custom `start`/`end`).

claudemeter.tokenLimit.description: Context window token limit override (0 = auto-detect from model and plan). Set manually to force a specific limit.

claudemeter.tokenOnlyMode.description: Token-only mode: Only track Claude Code tokens, skip Claude.ai web usage scraping entirely

claudemeter.useLegacyScraper.description: Use legacy browser-based scraper instead of streamlined HTTP fetching. The default HTTP method calls undocumented Claude.ai API endpoints that could change without notice. Enable this fallback if the HTTP method stops working due to API changes. Requires a Chromium-based browser (Chrome, Edge, Brave, etc.).

claudemeter.debugLogFile.description: Path to debug log file. Auto-populated on first run. Supports ~ for home directory.

claudemeter.debugLogMaxSizeKB.description: Maximum debug log file size in KB. Oldest entries are trimmed when exceeded (64-2048 KB, default 256 KB)
